species: Denitrifying/fermentative enrichment community (Wadden Sea sediment; fermentative Vibrionales/Photobacterium enriched)

growth_records.medium: Defined anoxic marine medium (glucose+acetate+7 amino acids, nitrite/nitrate)

up.method: D x influent = 0.08/day x 37.5 C-mM = 3.0 C-mM/day

sec.compound: fermentation products (acetate, butyrate, propionate, succinate, lactate)
sec.method: HPLC/GC; up to 3.1 mM total fermentation products detected (cross-feeding at 10 C)

growth_records.cite: Anna Hanke et al. Selective Pressure of Temperature on Competition and Cross-Feeding within Denitrifying and Fermentative Microbial Communities. Frontiers in Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2015.01461. [PMC4703780]
growth_records.method: calculated: dilution rate eventually 0.08 volume changes/day = 0.08/24 = 0.0033 h-1; mu=D at steady state; td=ln2/0.0033=208 h
growth_records.snippet: Eventually, the dilution rate was 0.08 volume changes/day and the C:N ratio was 1.8 C-mol/N-mol. Fermentation products (acetate, butyrate, propionate, succinate, lactate, up to 3.1 mM total) were detected in the culture... 37.5 C-mM for the
growth_records.notes: MIXED enrichment community, NOT single strain. 10 C chemostat, anoxic, pH 8.0. Denitrification unstable/incomplete at low T; required lowering D to 0.08 vol/day and raising carbon (37.5 C-mM). mu=D=0.08/24=0.0033 h-1; td=208 h. Volumetric carbon uptake 3.0 C-mM/day, nitrite ~1.52 mM/day (derived from D x influent). Fermentation products (acetate/butyrate/propionate/succinate/lactate) up to 3.1 mM total accumulated (fermentative Vibrionales/Photobacterium cross-feeding denitrifiers). No per-gDW rates.
